# UC-22.13.21 · Vendor Interactive Remote Access — Encryption and Session Recording Evidence

## Description

We verify vendor interactive remote access uses approved encryption (for example TLS versions on VPN or SSH) and that PAM session recording identifiers exist for the same sessions.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes network, pam and sourcetypes cyberark:ptalog`, cyberark:session_recording`, pan:globalprotect. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Map GlobalProtect or SSL VPN field names to `tls_version`. (2) Run a companion saved search on `index=pam` for vendor sessions missing `recording_id` in `cyberark:session_recording`. (3) Alert on weak TLS or missing recording for vendor users. (4) Document compensating controls for legacy jump hosts. Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog``, `sourcetype=cyberark:session_recording``, `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`, `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect` or SSL VPN logs, `index=pam` `sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog`, `sourcetype=cyberark:session_recording`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map GlobalProtect or SSL VPN field names to `tls_version`. (2) Run a companion saved search on `index=pam` for vendor sessions missing `recording_id` in `cyberark:session_recording`. (3) Alert on weak TLS or missing recording for vendor users. (4) Document compensating controls for legacy jump hosts.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:globalprotect earliest=-24h
| eval tls_version=coalesce(tls_version,ssl_tls_version)
| eval tls_ok=if(match(tls_version,"TLSv1\.[23]"),1,0)
| where tls_ok=0 AND isnotnull(user)
| stats count by user, public_ip, tls_version, gateway
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Vendor Interactive Remote Access — Encryption and Session Recording Evidence (CIP-005-6 R3 Part 3.2)** — We verify vendor interactive remote access uses approved encryption (for example TLS versions on VPN or SSH) and that PAM session recording identifiers exist for the same sessions.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect` or SSL VPN logs, `index=pam` `sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog`, `sourcetype=cyberark:session_recording`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: pan:globalprotect. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype=pan:globalprotect, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **tls_version** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **tls_ok** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where tls_ok=0 AND isnotnull(user)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, public_ip, tls_version, gateway** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with CyberArk PVWA / session recording for the same user and time window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (encryption and recording gaps), Timeline, Single value (violation count)

## SPL

```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:globalprotect earliest=-24h
| eval tls_version=coalesce(tls_version,ssl_tls_version)
| eval tls_ok=if(match(tls_version,"TLSv1\.[23]"),1,0)
| where tls_ok=0 AND isnotnull(user)
| stats count by user, public_ip, tls_version, gateway
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (encryption and recording gaps), Timeline, Single value (violation count). Key fields: `gateway`, `public_ip`, `tls_ok`, `tls_version`, `user`.
